data.content:
### Configure Playwright with NUnit using .runsettings XML

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-csharp.md

This XML configuration for a `.runsettings` file demonstrates how to set Playwright-related options specifically for the NUnit test runner. It includes defining the number of NUnit test workers, enabling verbose Playwright API logs via environment variables, and configuring browser launch options like browser name, timeout, and headless mode.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- NUnit adapter -->  
  <NUnit>
    <NumberOfTestWorkers>24</NumberOfTestWorkers>
  </NUnit>
  <!-- General run configuration -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <EnvironmentVariables>
      <!-- For debugging selectors, it's recommend to set the following environment variable -->
      <DEBUG>pw:api</DEBUG>
    </EnvironmentVariables>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <!-- Playwright -->  
  <Playwright>
    <BrowserName>chromium</BrowserName>
    <ExpectTimeout>5000</ExpectTimeout>
    <LaunchOptions>
      <Headless>false</Headless>
      <Channel>msedge</Channel>
    </LaunchOptions>
  </Playwright>
</RunSettings>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright with xUnit using .runsettings XML

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-csharp.md

This XML configuration for a `.runsettings` file demonstrates how to set Playwright-related options for xUnit and xUnit v3 test runners. It includes configuring maximum parallel threads for xUnit, enabling verbose Playwright API logs via environment variables, and defining browser launch options like browser name, timeout, and headless mode.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- See https://xunit.net/docs/runsettings -->
  <xUnit>
    <MaxParallelThreads>1</MaxParallelThreads>
  </xUnit>
  <!-- General run configuration -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <EnvironmentVariables>
      <!-- For debugging selectors, it's recommend to set the following environment variable -->
      <DEBUG>pw:api</DEBUG>
    </EnvironmentVariables>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <!-- Playwright -->  
  <Playwright>
    <BrowserName>chromium</BrowserName>
    <ExpectTimeout>5000</ExpectTimeout>
    <LaunchOptions>
      <Headless>false</Headless>
      <Channel>msedge</Channel>
    </LaunchOptions>
  </Playwright>
</RunSettings>
```

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- See https://xunit.net/docs/runsettings -->
  <xUnit>
    <MaxParallelThreads>1</MaxParallelThreads>
  </xUnit>
  <!-- General run configuration -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <EnvironmentVariables>
      <!-- For debugging selectors, it's recommend to set the following environment variable -->
      <DEBUG>pw:api</DEBUG>
    </EnvironmentVariables>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <!-- Playwright -->  
  <Playwright>
    <BrowserName>chromium</BrowserName>
    <ExpectTimeout>5000</ExpectTimeout>
    <LaunchOptions>
      <Headless>false</Headless>
      <Channel>msedge</Channel>
    </LaunchOptions>
  </Playwright>
</RunSettings>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright Test Runner with .runsettings XML

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-csharp.md

Provides an example of a `.runsettings` XML file for configuring Playwright test runners (e.g., `Microsoft.Playwright.NUnit`, `Microsoft.Playwright.MSTest`). This configuration allows setting browser name, expect timeouts, launch options like headless mode, and environment variables for debugging selectors.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Playwright -->
  <Playwright>
    <BrowserName>chromium</BrowserName>
    <ExpectTimeout>5000</ExpectTimeout>
    <LaunchOptions>
      <Headless>true</Headless>
      <Channel>msedge</Channel>
    </LaunchOptions>
  </Playwright>
  <!-- General run configuration -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <EnvironmentVariables>
      <!-- For debugging selectors, it's recommend to set the following environment variable -->
      <DEBUG>pw:api</DEBUG>
    </EnvironmentVariables>
  </RunConfiguration>
</RunSettings>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Basic Playwright Test Runner Options (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-configuration-js.md

Defines fundamental settings for Playwright tests, including the test directory, parallel execution, CI-specific behaviors like retries and `forbidOnly`, reporter type, base URL for `page.goto()`, and browser-specific project configurations. It also shows how to integrate a local development server.

```javascript
import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  // Look for test files in the "tests" directory, relative to this configuration file.
  testDir: 'tests',

  // Run all tests in parallel.
  fullyParallel: true,

  // Fail the build on CI if you accidentally left test.only in the source code.
  forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,

  // Retry on CI only.
  retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0,

  // Opt out of parallel tests on CI.
  workers: process.env.CI ? 1 : undefined,

  // Reporter to use
  reporter: 'html',

  use: {
    // Base URL to use in actions like `await page.goto('/')`.
    baseURL: 'http://localhost:3000',

    // Collect trace when retrying the failed test.
    trace: 'on-first-retry',
  },
  // Configure projects for major browsers.
  projects: [
    {
      name: 'chromium',
      use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] },
    },
  ],
  // Run your local dev server before starting the tests.
  webServer: {
    command: 'npm run start',
    url: 'http://localhost:3000',
    reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure MSTest Parallel Test Workers for Playwright .NET

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-csharp.md

This snippet shows how to specify the number of worker processes for parallel test execution in MSTest with Playwright for .NET. The MSTest.Parallelize.Workers parameter is used with dotnet test, with an option to include a .runsettings file for configuration.

```bash
dotnet test --settings:.runsettings -- MSTest.Parallelize.Workers=4
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright with MSTest using .runsettings XML

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-csharp.md

This XML configuration for a `.runsettings` file demonstrates how to set Playwright-related options specifically for the MSTest runner. It includes configuring MSTest parallelization workers and scope, enabling verbose Playwright API logs via environment variables, and defining browser launch options such as browser name, timeout, and headless mode.

```xml
<RunSettings>
  <!-- MSTest adapter -->  
  <MSTest>
    <Parallelize>
      <Workers>4</Workers>
      <Scope>ClassLevel</Scope>
    </Parallelize>
  </MSTest>
  <!-- General run configuration -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <EnvironmentVariables>
      <!-- For debugging selectors, it's recommend to set the following environment variable -->
      <DEBUG>pw:api</DEBUG>
    </EnvironmentVariables>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <!-- Playwright -->  
  <Playwright>
    <BrowserName>chromium</BrowserName>
    <ExpectTimeout>5000</ExpectTimeout>
    <LaunchOptions>
      <Headless>false</Headless>
      <Channel>msedge</Channel>
    </LaunchOptions>
  </Playwright>
</RunSettings>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Parallel Playwright Test Execution with Multiple Workers (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-csharp.md

These commands demonstrate how to configure different .NET test runners (MSTest, NUnit, xUnit) to execute Playwright tests in parallel using multiple worker processes. This significantly reduces total test execution time for large test suites.

```bash
dotnet test -- NUnit.NumberOfTestWorkers=5
```

```bash
dotnet test -- MSTest.Parallelize.Workers=5
```

```bash
dotnet test -- xUnit.MaxParallelThreads=5
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright Project with Gradle

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-java.md

This snippet provides Gradle build configurations in both Groovy and Kotlin DSL for a Playwright project. It defines dependencies, an application main class, and a custom `playwright` task to run Playwright CLI tools. It also sets up JUnit Platform for test execution.

```groovy
plugins {
  application
  id 'java'
}

repositories {
  mavenCentral()
}

dependencies {
  implementation 'com.microsoft.playwright:playwright:%%VERSION%%'
}

application {
  mainClass = 'org.example.App'
}

// Usage: ./gradlew playwright --args="help"
task playwright(type: JavaExec) {
  classpath sourceSets.test.runtimeClasspath
  mainClass = 'com.microsoft.playwright.CLI'
}

test {
  useJUnitPlatform()
}
```

```kotlin
plugins {
  application
  id("java")
}

repositories {
  mavenCentral()
}

dependencies {
  implementation("com.microsoft.playwright:playwright:%%VERSION%%")
}

application {
  mainClass.set("org.example.App")
}

// Usage: ./gradlew playwright --args="help"
tasks.register<JavaExec>("playwright") {
  classpath(sourceSets["test"].runtimeClasspath)
  mainClass.set("com.microsoft.playwright.CLI")
}

tasks.test {
  useJUnitPlatform()
  testLogging {
    events("passed", "skipped", "failed")
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure NUnit Parallel Test Workers for Playwright .NET

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-csharp.md

This snippet demonstrates how to adjust the number of worker processes for parallel test execution when using NUnit with Playwright for .NET. It utilizes the NUnit.NumberOfTestWorkers parameter passed to the dotnet test command to control concurrency.

```bash
dotnet test -- NUnit.NumberOfTestWorkers=5
```

--------------------------------

### Configure xUnit Max Parallel Threads for Playwright .NET

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-csharp.md

This snippet illustrates how to set the maximum number of parallel threads for xUnit tests when using Playwright for .NET. The xUnit.MaxParallelThreads parameter is passed via the dotnet test command to control test parallelism.

```bash
dotnet test -- xUnit.MaxParallelThreads=5
```
